[Workflow Task from Orchestrator]
Workflow ID: 2dffd527-066
Subtask ID: 9ed79d34

Task: Create a bash script that monitors disk usage and alerts when any partition exceeds 90%. Requirements:

3. Include the partition name, usage percentage, and available space in the alert
4. Return appropriate exit codes (0 for OK, 1 for alert triggered)
5. Make it suitable for running as a cron job
6. Include comments and make it production-ready

Save it to ~/scripts/disk-monitor.sh and make it executable.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# =============================================================================
# Disk Usage Monitor Script
# =============================================================================
# Monitors all mounted partitions and alerts when any partition exceeds
# a specified threshold (default: 90%).
#
# Usage:
#   ./disk-monitor.sh [THRESHOLD]
#
# Arguments:
#   THRESHOLD - Optional usage percentage threshold (default: 90)
#
# Exit Codes:
#   0 - All partitions below threshold (OK)
#   1 - One or more partitions exceeded threshold
#
# Examples:
#   ./disk-monitor.sh           # Check with default 90% threshold
#   ./disk-monitor.sh 80        # Check with 80% threshold
#   ./disk-monitor.sh 2>/dev/null  # Suppress stderr output
#
# Cron Usage:
#   * * * * * /home/user/scripts/disk-monitor.sh 90 >> /var/log/disk-monitor.log 2>&1
#
# =============================================================================

set -euo pipefail

# Configuration
readonly DEFAULT_THRESHOLD=90
readonly SCRIPT_NAME="$(basename "$0")"

# Threshold from argument or default
THRESHOLD="${1:-$DEFAULT_THRESHOLD}"

# Validate threshold is a number between 1-100
if ! [[ "$THRESHOLD" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]] || [ "$THRESHOLD" -lt 1 ] || [ "$THRESHOLD" -gt 100 ]; then
    echo "Error: Threshold must be a number between 1 and 100" >&2
    exit 2
fi

# Initialize alert flag
ALERT_TRIGGERED=0

# Get current timestamp
TIMESTAMP=$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')

# Header output (to stdout for logging)
echo "[$TIMESTAMP] Disk usage check started (threshold: ${THRESHOLD}%)"
echo "================================================================="

# Parse df output excluding pseudo filesystems
# Fields: Filesystem, Size, Used, Available, Use%, Mounted on
df -H -x tmpfs -x devtmpfs -x squashfs -x ramfs -x overlay 2>/dev/null | \
    tail -n +2 | while read -r filesystem size used available usage mountpoint; do
    
    # Extract percentage as number (remove % suffix)
    usage_pct="${usage%%%}"
    
    # Skip if usage is not a valid number (e.g., for some special filesystems)
    if ! [[ "$usage_pct" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]; then
        continue
    fi
    
    # Check if usage exceeds threshold
    if [ "$usage_pct" -gt "$THRESHOLD" ]; then
        # Alert output to both stdout and stderr
        ALERT_MSG="[ALERT] $mountpoint on $filesystem is at ${usage_pct}% (threshold: ${THRESHOLD}%) - Available: $available"
        echo "$ALERT_MSG"
        echo "$ALERT_MSG" >&2
    fi
done

# Alternative: Single-pass check that also captures exit code
ALERT_COUNT=0
while IFS= read -r line; do
    # Skip header
    [[ -z "$line" ]] && continue
    
    # Parse fields
    filesystem=$(echo "$line" | awk '{print $1}')
    usage=$(echo "$line" | awk '{print $5}')
    available=$(echo "$line" | awk '{print $4}')
    mountpoint=$(echo "$line" | awk '{for(i=6;i<=NF;i++) printf "%s ", $i; print ""}' | sed 's/ $//')
    
    # Clean up fields
    [[ -z "$filesystem" ]] && continue
    
    # Skip pseudo filesystems
    [[ "$filesystem" =~ ^(tmpfs|devtmpfs|squashfs|ramfs|overlay|devfs|proc)$ ]] && continue
    
    # Extract percentage
    usage_pct="${usage%%%}"
    
    # Skip if not a valid number
    if ! [[ "$usage_pct" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]; then
        continue
    fi
    
    # Check threshold
    if [ "$usage_pct" -gt "$THRESHOLD" ]; then
        ALERT_COUNT=$((ALERT_COUNT + 1))
        {
            echo "================================================================="
            echo "DISK SPACE ALERT - $TIMESTAMP"
            echo "================================================================="
            echo "  Partition: $filesystem"
            echo "  Mount:     $mountpoint"
            echo "  Usage:     ${usage_pct}% (threshold: ${THRESHOLD}%)"
            echo "  Available: $available"
            echo "================================================================="
        } | tee /dev/stderr
    fi
done < <(df -H -x tmpfs -x devtmpfs -x squashfs -x ramfs -x overlay 2>/dev/null | tail -n +2)

# Summary
if [ "$ALERT_COUNT" -gt 0 ]; then
    echo ""
    echo "[$TIMESTAMP] WARNING: $ALERT_COUNT partition(s) exceeded ${THRESHOLD}% threshold"
    exit 1
else
    echo "[$TIMESTAMP] OK: All partitions below ${THRESHOLD}% threshold"
    exit 0
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# =============================================================================
# Disk Usage Monitor Script
# =============================================================================
# Monitors all mounted partitions and alerts when any partition exceeds
# a specified threshold (default: 90%).
#
# Usage:
#   ./disk-monitor.sh [THRESHOLD]
#
# Arguments:
#   THRESHOLD - Optional usage percentage threshold (default: 90)
#
# Exit Codes:
#   0 - All partitions below threshold (OK)
#   1 - One or more partitions exceeded threshold
#   2 - Invalid arguments
#
# Examples:
#   ./disk-monitor.sh           # Check with default 90% threshold
#   ./disk-monitor.sh 80        # Check with 80% threshold
#   ./disk-monitor.sh 2>/dev/null  # Suppress stderr output
#
# Cron Usage:
#   * * * * * /home/user/scripts/disk-monitor.sh 90 >> /var/log/disk-monitor.log 2>&1
#
# =============================================================================

set -euo pipefail

# Configuration
readonly DEFAULT_THRESHOLD=90

# Threshold from argument or default
THRESHOLD="${1:-$DEFAULT_THRESHOLD}"

# Validate threshold is a number between 1-100
if ! [[ "$THRESHOLD" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]] || [ "$THRESHOLD" -lt 1 ] || [ "$THRESHOLD" -gt 100 ]; then
    echo "Error: Threshold must be a number between 1 and 100" >&2
    exit 2
fi

# Get current timestamp
TIMESTAMP=$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')

# Temporary file to capture output
TMPFILE=$(mktemp)
trap "rm -f $TMPFILE" EXIT

# Counter for exceeded partitions
ALERT_COUNT=0

# Header output
echo "[$TIMESTAMP] Disk usage check started (threshold: ${THRESHOLD}%)"
echo "================================================================="

# Parse df output - skip pseudo filesystems
# Get filesystem data in a portable way
while IFS= read -r line; do
    # Skip empty lines
    [[ -z "$line" ]] && continue
    
    # Parse the line - handle various df formats
    filesystem=$(echo "$line" | awk '{print $1}')
    usage=$(echo "$line" | awk '{print $5}')
    available=$(echo "$line" | awk '{print $4}')
    # Mount point is everything from field 6 to end (may contain spaces)
    mountpoint=$(echo "$line" | cut -d' ' -f6-)
    
    # Skip if filesystem is empty or invalid
    [[ -z "$filesystem" ]] && continue
    
    # Skip pseudo filesystems by name pattern
    case "$filesystem" in
        tmpfs|devtmpfs|squashfs|ramfs|overlay|devfs|proc|sysfs|cgroup*|devfs)
            continue
            ;;
        *)
            ;;
    esac
    
    # skip if no usage percentage
    [[ -z "$usage" ]] && continue
    
    # Extract percentage as number (remove % suffix)
    usage_pct="${usage%%\%}"
    
    # Skip if not a valid number
    if ! [[ "$usage_pct" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]; then
        continue
    fi
    
    # Check threshold
    if [ "$usage_pct" -gt "$THRESHOLD" ]; then
        ALERT_COUNT=$((ALERT_COUNT + 1))
        {
            echo ""
            echo "DISK SPACE ALERT"
            echo "  Partition: $filesystem"
            echo "  Mount:     $mountpoint"
            echo "  Usage:     ${usage_pct}% (exceeds ${THRESHOLD}%)"
            echo "  Available: $available"
        } | tee -a "$TMPFILE"
    fi
done < <(df -H 2>/dev/null | tail -n +2)

# Add separator if there were any alerts
if [ -s "$TMPFILE" ]; then
    echo "" >> "$TMPFILE"
fi

# Output to stderr
if [ -s "$TMPFILE" ]; then
    cat "$TMPFILE" >&2
fi

# Summary and exit
echo ""
if [ "$ALERT_COUNT" -gt 0 ]; then
    echo "[$TIMESTAMP] WARNING: $ALERT_COUNT partition(s) exceeded ${THRESHOLD}% threshold"
    exit 1
else
    echo "[$TIMESTAMP] OK: All partitions below ${THRESHOLD}% threshold"
    exit 0
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# =============================================================================
# Disk Usage Monitor Script
# =============================================================================
# Monitors all mounted partitions and alerts when any partition exceeds
# a specified threshold (default: 90%).
#
# Usage:
#   ./disk-monitor.sh [THRESHOLD]
#
# Arguments:
#   THRESHOLD - Optional usage percentage threshold (default: 90)
#
# Exit Codes:
#   0 - All partitions below threshold (OK)
#   1 - One or more partitions exceeded threshold
#   2 - Invalid arguments
#
# Examples:
#   ./disk-monitor.sh              # Check with default 90% threshold
#   ./disk-monitor.sh 80           # Check with 80% threshold
#   ./disk-monitor.sh 2>/dev/null  # Suppress stderr output
#
# Cron Installation:
#   */5 * * * * /home/user/scripts/disk-monitor.sh >> /var/log/disk-monitor.log 2>&1
#
# =============================================================================

set -euo pipefail

# Configuration
readonly DEFAULT_THRESHOLD=90
readonly VERSION="1.0.0"

# Show help
if [[ "${1:-}" == "-h" || "${1:-}" == "--help" ]]; then
    echo "Disk Usage Monitor v$VERSION"
    echo "Usage: $0 [THRESHOLD]"
    echo ""
    echo "Arguments:"
    echo "  THRESHOLD - Usage percentage threshold (1-100, default: 90)"
    echo ""
    echo "Exit codes:"
    echo "  0 - All partitions below threshold"
    echo "  1 - One or more partitions exceeded threshold"
    echo "  2 - Invalid arguments"
    exit 0
fi

# Threshold from argument or default
THRESHOLD="${1:-$DEFAULT_THRESHOLD}"

# Validate threshold is a number between 1-100
if ! [[ "$THRESHOLD" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]] || [ "$THRESHOLD" -lt 1 ] || [ "$THRESHOLD" -gt 100 ]; then
    echo "Error: Threshold must be a number between 1 and 100" >&2
    exit 2
fi

# Get current timestamp
TIMESTAMP=$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')

# Initialize alert counter
ALERT_COUNT=0

# Header
{
    echo "[$TIMESTAMP] Disk usage check started (threshold: ${THRESHOLD}%)"
    echo "================================================================="
} | tee /dev/stderr

# Parse df output
# Get filesystem data
while IFS= read -r line; do
    # Skip empty lines
    [[ -z "$line" ]] && continue
    
    # Parse fields: Filesystem Size Used Avail Use% Mount
    filesystem=$(echo "$line" | awk '{print $1}')
    available=$(echo "$line" | awk '{print $4}')
    usage=$(echo "$line" | awk '{print $5}')
    mountpoint=$(echo "$line" | cut -d' ' -f6-)
    
    # Skip if filesystem is empty
    [[ -z "$filesystem" ]] && continue
    
    # Skip pseudo filesystems
    case "$filesystem" in
        tmpfs|devtmpfs|squashfs|ramfs|overlay|devfs|proc|sysfs|cgroup*|debugfs|tracefs|hugetlbfs|mqueue|securityfs|pstore|bpf|configfs)
            continue
            ;;
    esac
    
    # Skip App Translocation volumes (macOS specific)
    [[ "$mountpoint" == *"AppTranslocation"* ]] && continue
    
    # Skip if no usage percentage
    [[ -z "$usage" ]] && continue
    
    # Extract percentage as number (remove % suffix)
    usage_pct="${usage%%\%}"
    
    # Skip if not a valid number
    if ! [[ "$usage_pct" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]; then
        continue
    fi
    
    # Check threshold
    if [ "$usage_pct" -gt "$THRESHOLD" ]; then
        ALERT_COUNT=$((ALERT_COUNT + 1))
        
        # Build alert message
        MSG="DISK SPACE ALERT
  Partition: $filesystem
  Mount:     $mountpoint
  Usage:     ${usage_pct}% (threshold: ${THRESHOLD}%)
  Available: $available"""
        
        # Output to both stdout and stderr
        echo ""
        echo "$MSG" | tee /dev/stderr
    fi
done < <(df -H 2>/dev/null | tail -n +2)

# Summary
echo ""
if [ "$ALERT_COUNT" -gt 0 ]; then
    SUMMARY="[$TIMESTAMP] WARNING: $ALERT_COUNT partition(s) exceeded ${THRESHOLD}% threshold"
    echo "$SUMMARY" | tee /dev/stderr
    exit 1
else
    SUMMARY="[$TIMESTAMP] OK: All partitions below ${THRESHOLD}% threshold"
    echo "$SUMMARY"
    exit 0
fi
